concepts.name: Definite Integral
concepts.short_definition: A mathematical operation that represents the accumulation of quantities, often visualized as the area under a curve.

concepts.name: Antiderivative
concepts.short_definition: A function whose derivative is the original function.

concepts.name: Fundamental Theorem of Calculus
concepts.short_definition: Connects the concept of differentiation and integration, stating that integration is the inverse operation of differentiation.

concepts.name: Riemann Sums
concepts.short_definition: A method of approximating the area under a curve by dividing it into a series of rectangles.

concepts.name: Limit of Riemann Sums
concepts.short_definition: The value that Riemann sums approach as the number of rectangles approaches infinity, yielding the exact area.

concepts.name: Integration Techniques
concepts.short_definition: Various strategies and rules used to find antiderivatives of complex functions.

concepts.name: Area Between Curves
concepts.short_definition: Calculating the area of a region bounded by two or more functions.

concepts.name: Numerical Integration
concepts.short_definition: Techniques for approximating definite integrals when analytical solutions are difficult or impossible.

concepts.name: Applications of Integration
concepts.short_definition: Using integration to solve problems in geometry, physics, economics, and other fields.

concepts.name: Improper Integrals
concepts.short_definition: Definite integrals where the interval of integration is infinite or the integrand has an infinite discontinuity.
